commits.title: feat: add quotation-automation-system workspace to projects.json
commits.description: Created add-workspace.py script for adding workspaces to VPS projects.json. Updated create-projects-json.py to include quotation-automation-system. Deployed to VPS and restarted superroo-api.

commits.title: fix: Telegram bot not responding in group chats + 404 login URL
commits.description:
Two bugs fixed:

1. Telegram bot now responds in group chats: refactored bot.ts to factory pattern (createBot) with proper group chat support including bot_command entity detection, @mention detection, reply-to-bot detection, my_chat_member handling (bot added to group), and configurable authorization per chat type. Added 35 tests covering all scenarios.

2. Login 404: Changed CLOUD_APP_LOGIN and related URLs from app.superroo.com/sign-in to clerk.superroo.com/sign-in since the Clerk auth pages are hosted at clerk.superroo.com, not app.superroo.com.
bugsFixed: Telegram bot not responding in group chats
404 error when logging in via Telegram

commits.title: feat: rewrite Docker tab with full Docker Control Center UI
fix: Telegram group chat auth not recognizing logged-in user + /projects command missing
commits.description:
Four bugs fixed:

1. /projects command was missing from command routing in telegramBot.js — the handleProjects function existed but was never called in the if/else chain, so /projects fell through to NL processing which failed.

2. handleTelegramProjects() in auth.js threw 'Not authenticated' error instead of returning gracefully when no session existed, causing /projects to crash for unauthenticated users.

3. handleSpecify failed because handleTelegramProjects called getTelegramSession with group chatId, but the session was created in DM with a different chatId — getTelegramSession had no fallback to try without chatId.

4. getTelegramSession() in auth.js lacked the same two-step fallback that checkAuthSession() in telegramBot.js had (try exact chatId match first, then fall back to most recent session).
bugsFixed: Telegram /projects command not routed in command handler
Telegram group chat auth not recognizing DM session
Telegram /specify fails in group chat due to chatId mismatch

commits.title: fix: 6 Telegram bugs found during E2E testing — messageId undefined, malformed markdown, missing commands, Mini IDE callbacks, engineer sanitization, notifier fallback
commits.description:
Six bugs fixed during comprehensive E2E testing:

1. Bug 5: 10 commands missing from command routing (/miniide, /workspace, /session, /settings, /agents, /code, /diff, /approve, /deploy, /status) — added all routes.

2. Bug 6: messageId is not defined in handleNaturalLanguageInstruction debug_plan handler — removed reference to undefined messageId variable.

3. Bug 7: Malformed markdown causing Telegram API 'can't parse entities' errors — added fallback to plain text in sendMessage() when markdown parsing fails.

4. Bug 8: Mini IDE callback_data 'projects' and 'help' not handled — added handlers for both callback types.

5. Bug 9: telegramEngineer.js wraps log content in backticks without sanitization — added sanitizeForCode() and sanitizeForCodeBlock() helpers.

6. Bug 10: telegramNotifier.js lacks markdown fallback in sendInlineKeyboard() and editMessageText() — added retry with parse_mode: '' fallback.

7. Bug 11: askAI() timeout error message unhelpful — improved to show provider names and distinguish timeout errors.

8. Added structured error logging ([ace-error], [ace-warn], [ace-usage]) throughout all command handlers for Ace Team monitoring.

9. Fixed syntax error in handleNaturalLanguageInstruction — broken nested try/catch structure.
bugsFixed: 10 missing commands not routed in command handler
messageId is not defined in debug_plan handler
Malformed markdown causes Telegram API 400 errors
Mini IDE callback_data 'projects' and 'help' not handled
telegramEngineer.js lacks markdown sanitization for log content
telegramNotifier.js lacks markdown fallback in sendInlineKeyboard/editMessageText
Syntax error in handleNaturalLanguageInstruction try/catch nesting

commits.title: feat: enable coding everywhere — Mini IDE AI commands, dashboard auth, real IDE Terminal backend
commits.description:
Three fixes to enable coding from all platforms (Telegram, Cloud Dashboard IDE Terminal, Mini IDE):

1. Mini IDE AI Commands (server.js:386): Removed the SUPERROO_API_KEY requirement and localhost check so AI commands forward to the local SuperRoo API even when URL is localhost.

2. Mini IDE Auth (app.js:45-69): Added dashboard token support — when no Telegram initData is available (browser access), reads localStorage.getItem('superroo_auth_token') and sends it as a Bearer token.

3. IDE Terminal Backend (api.js:1879-2158): Replaced all simulated/mock IDE workspace routes with real implementations — file tree walks actual filesystem, terminal executes real commands, chat calls real AI providers, pipeline updates real state, GitHub import clones real repos.

4. Auth Bypass (auth.js:891-892): Added bypass for /ide-workspace/ paths in handleAuthRoute so they fall through to api.js handlers instead of being intercepted by requireAuth.

deploys.failureReason: Deploy pipeline was interrupted before completion — status was stuck as 'building' for over 1 year. Marked as failed by deployer agent upgrade.
